business.headline: Market open higher as auto stocks lead; monsoon worries keep gains in checkIndian benchmark indices opened higher as auto stocks led a recovery from Tuesday's losses. Gains stayed limited as monsoon concerns and stalled US-Iran talks kept investors cautious.

Gold prices fall, but buyers disappear as demand drops 70%Gold demand in India has fallen by over 70%, as higher import duty, subdued buying sentiment and a sharp correction in prices keep consumers away. With MCX gold down over Rs 50,000 from its record high, here's why demand has slumped and what experts expect next.

Nayara Energy cuts petrol price by Rs 5, diesel by Rs 3 as global oil rates coolNayara Energy has cut petrol prices by Rs 5 per litre and diesel by Rs 3 per litre across its nationwide retail network, becoming the first fuel retailer to reduce prices in over two years. The move follows a decline in global crude oil prices after easing tensions in West Asia.

Insurance mis-selling: IRDAI wants every policy linked to person who sold itIRDAI has proposed tagging every insurance policy to the individual who sold it instead of only the bank or intermediary. The move aims to improve accountability in insurance mis-selling cases and strengthen consumer protection.

Can you claim your EPF pension before 58? Here's what every employee must knowEarly retirement is becoming increasingly common, but what happens to your EPF pension if you stop working before 58? Can you start receiving it right away? Let us find out.

Price of 19-kg commercial LPG cylinder reduced by Rs 183. Check new ratesThe government has slashed the price of 19-kg commercial LPG cylinders by Rs 183 in a big relief to customers after multiple hikes due to the West Asia crisis.

Trump earned over $1.4 billion from crypto ventures in 2025, filings showA law from 1978 mandates the President and the Vice President of the US to declare their income as well as their assets. Trump's latest filings detailed the fact that his companies received almost USD 800 million from World Liberty Financial, a crypto venture he and his sons co-founded.

Centre hikes special duty on petrol, cuts levy on diesel, aviation fuel exportsThe Centre has raised the windfall tax on petrol exports while cutting levies on diesel and ATF from July 1. The revision also widens export duty exemptions and leaves domestic fuel excise rates unchanged.

RBI says banks' bad loans hit multi-decade low of 1.8%, warns of AI cyberattacksDespite repeated shocks, the global financial system has thus far demonstrated notable resilience, with markets remaining orderly after an initial bout of volatility following the outbreak of the West Asia conflict, the RBI said in its Financial Stability Report (FSR).

Kotak Mahindra to buy Deutsche Bank's retail business in India for $30 millionThe acquisition will help Kotak expand its presence among retail and affluent customers, while Deutsche Bank will focus on its core business areas.

UK says it's minded to intervene in $110-billion Paramount-Warner Bros mergerUK Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy said the proposed merger could have implications for Britain's media landscape and that she was considering intervention on public interest grounds.

Govt extends customs duty relief on key petrochemical imports until July 15Although the situation in West Asia has started to improve, the government has chosen to continue the customs duty exemption for a short period to help industries adjust without facing sudden cost pressures.

Noel Tata to step down as Voltas Chairman, announces decision at AGMDuring the AGM, Noel Tata said the company has built a strong leadership team by promoting talent from within and bringing in experienced professionals from outside.
Govt extends CBDT chairman Ravi Agrawal's term by six monthsRavi Agrawal's new term will begin on July 1, 2026, and continue till December 31, 2026, or until further orders, whichever comes earlier.

Sensex ends 250 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; Maruti Suzuki gains 5%The BSE Sensex fell 249.70 points, or 0.33%, to close at 76,478.67, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 80.50 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 23,865.75.

Centre pushes GST Appellate Tribunal appeal filing deadline by 1 monthThe new deadline is July 31, 2026, replacing the earlier deadline of June 30, 2026, which had been notified in September last year.

Indians love gold. So why is there a rush to sell it?Gold still occupies a special place in Indian households, but rising prices and changing investor behaviour are transforming it from a family heirloom into a modern financial asset.

8th Pay Commission: Salary and allowance data to be submitted ahead of pay revisionThe information is expected to help the Commission prepare recommendations on revised pay scales, allowances, overall government expenditure and long-term financial sustainability.

Filed your ITR? Complete this step within 30 days of filing or it may be rejectedClicked 'Submit' on your ITR? Don't relax just yet. You still need to complete one crucial step to make your tax return valid. Have a look.

Guaranteed returns, zero risk: How $60 million investment scam fooled 300 peoplePaul Regan pleaded guilty after prosecutors accused him of running a $60 million Ponzi scheme that drew more than 300 US investors with promises of guaranteed returns and zero risk. The case shows how emotional reassurance, false protection claims and trust can override basic questions about investment risk.

entertainment.headline: Lock Upp 2: Influencer Shreya Kalra claims Akanksha Chamola is bisexual The latest episode of Lock Upp 2 saw another personal revelation involving contestant Akanksha Chamola. Days after announcing her separation from actor Gaurav Khanna, fellow contestant Shreya Kalra claimed to reveal her "secret" during the show.

Ahead of Aamir Khan's wedding, brother Faissal apologises to him, mother after feud Days before Aamir Khan's wedding to Gauri Spratt, the actor's brother Faissal Khan has apologised for the remarks he made against the family in public. He said rebuilding relationships now matters more to him than proving who was right or wrong.

Lock Upp 2: Ram Kapoor's 'I won't change' attitude leaves Farah Khan exasperated Filmmaker and host Farah Khan's confrontation with actor Ram Kapoor turned into a prickly on-air showdown on Lock Upp 2. His refusal to change his rude behaviour, even while being saved, became the episode's real spectacle.

SS Rajamouli shares big update on Varanasi, says 80-day shoot still pending Director SS Rajamouli said in France that Varanasi has about 80 days of shooting left. The update indicates the Mahesh Babu-led film remains on course for its grand April 2027 release.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 5: Film beats 2007 original's lifetime haul Welcome To The Jungle has crossed the lifetime worldwide gross of the 2007 original Welcome within five days of release. The Akshay Kumar-led comedy has earned Rs 120.12 crore worldwide, overtaking the first film's Rs 117.91 crore lifetime haul.

Maa Inti Bangaaram to Rao Bahadur: Is Telugu cinema entering a redefining phase? A cluster of Telugu releases, from Sing Geetham to Nagabandham, is spotlighting sharply different creative bets. Filmmakers say the trend points to a wider space for originality alongside big-scale commercial cinema.

Actor Kayadu Lohar recalls hitting eve-teaser with stone: His head started bleeding During Idhayam Murali promotions, actor Kayadu Lohar recalled throwing a stone at an eve-teaser during her school days. Her remarks framed the episode as a moment of fighting back instead of staying silent.

Band karo yeh sab: Neha Dhupia blasts paparazzi over inappropriate back shots Actor Neha Dhupia called out photographers at a Mumbai event over inappropriate back shots. Her remarks have reignited the conversation around respecting actors' boundaries and the need for more ethical paparazzi practices.

Marathi theatre legend, film director Vijaya Mehta dies at 91 Acclaimed theatre director, actor and producer Vijaya Mehta died on Tuesday night after prolonged ill health. Her work across theatre group Rangayan, Marathi experimental theatre and parallel cinema shaped generations of performers and directors.

Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding buzz grows after National Guard sighting at venue A viral video showed National Guard personnel outside Madison Square Garden ahead of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's expected July 3 wedding event. The sighting triggered security speculation online.

Choreographer Bosco Martis admitted to hospital after chest discomfort Bollywood choreographer Bosco Leslie Martis has been admitted to Breach Candy Hospital after chest congestion and discomfort. Doctors say his initial reports are normal and he remains under observation as a precaution.

Samantha's new baby bump pic comes with cheeky note: My six pack, I'll see you Samantha Ruth Prabhu shared a baby bump photo along with a note for her six-pack on Instagram. She is due for delivery later this year.

Timothee Chalamet and Selena Gomez voice rocket scientists in Not Alone trailer Illumination has released the first trailer for Not Alone, starring Timothee Chalamet and Selena Gomez. The preview sets up Chalamet's first animated voice role alongside a sci-fi chase involving three runaway aliens.

Actor Ansiba Hassan defamation case: FIR against Tini Tom over communal remarks An Ernakulam magistrate court has directed police to register a case against Tini Tom on actor Ansiba Hassan's complaint. The order follows her allegations of defamation and communal slurs.

Love is still the same: Gaurav Khanna breaks silence as Akanksha announces divorce Actor Gaurav Khanna has broken his silence after Akanksha Chamola confirmed their divorce on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. Here's what the actor said and why the couple decided to part ways.

Spider-Man Tom Holland takes Lionel Messi web-swinging in new video. Watch Sony Pictures has unveiled a surprise Spider-Man: Brand New Day video featuring Tom Holland's Peter Parker and Lionel Messi. Here's why the crossover has everyone talking.

Paer pe girke: Ssunita Ahuja reveals how Kashmera Shah mended family feud After years of estrangement, actor Govinda's wife Ssunita Ahuja revealed that Kashmera Shah apologised with tears during their emotional reunion, bringing the family's 14-year feud to an end.

Gaurav Khanna makes first post after wife Akanksha Chamola announces divorce Actor Gaurav Khanna shared a social media post after Akanksha Chamola announced their divorce on Lock Upp Season 2. Her remarks indicated they remain on speaking terms but differ on their future, including having children.

Half-yearly box office report: Dhurandhar leads a mixed report card at the movies Half-yearly box office report 2026: Dhurandhar dominates as trade experts analyse winners, flops, Hollywood's impact and what lies ahead.

general news.headline: Hid money in washrooms: What Ram Mandir donation theft accused told copsAfter receiving court permission, police interrogated Avinash Shukla for nearly two hours on Tuesday, during which he admitted to stealing crores of rupees and described the alleged sequence of events.

Stopped atomic destruction: Netanyahu warns of another strike on Iran if neededBenjamin Netanyahu said Israel could strike Iran again if needed as a US-Iran framework took effect. His remarks sharpened Israeli criticism of the deal even as Washington pushed both sides to hold fire.

Amazon sued for Prime Video ads, charging extra to remove themAmazon is facing legal trouble in Australia after introducing ads to Prime Video and asking subscribers to pay extra for an ad-free experience.

8 killed, 22 injured as bus rams truck, bursts into flames in Rajasthan's DausaEight people were killed and 22 injured after a Rishikesh-Indore passenger bus rammed a truck and caught fire on the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway in Rajasthan's Dausa at around 2:30 am. Around 37 passengers were on board. Police and rescue teams launched relief operations, while the cause is under investigation.

10 to 6 in office, AC bus and Hindu funds: How CM Vijay silenced his criticsFrom the day he entered politics, C Joseph Vijay was dismissed by his critics as inexperienced and unfit to govern. Yet, Tamil Nadu voted him to power. Just as his films opened to blockbuster success, his first moves as the chief minister have won him praise and silenced his critics.

How India still hasn't closed the borewell trapA four-year-old boy, Nirvair Singh, died after falling into an abandoned borewell in Ambala despite a 21-hour rescue. The tragedy has revived memories of Prince's 2006 rescue and renewed scrutiny of India's failure to seal unsafe borewells.

Mexico secure first knockout win in 40 years, oust Ecuador to reach last 16Azteca was rocking long before kick-off and the celebrations continued well after the final whistle as Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 to register their first FIFA World Cup knockout victory in 40 years and book a place in the last 16.

Pak warns world over Indus Waters Treaty at global conferencePakistan organised an international conference on Indus Waters and warned that if the treaty failed no world order that was on paper would remain secure. India put the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance after the terror attack in Pahalgam. Pakistan has since been desperate.

Ram Kapoor defends cheating in marriage. Is infidelity not a dealbreaker anymore?Ram Kapoor said a one-off act of infidelity need not end a marriage, while actress Akansha Chamola said cheating cannot be called a mistake. The exchange has reignited a wider debate on trust, boundaries and whether marriage can recover from betrayal.

Ready for war if diplomacy fails: Iran warns US ahead of high-stakes Doha talksIran's chief negotiator said Tehran remained committed to diplomacy but was "prepared for war" if negotiations failed, insisting its nuclear rights, including uranium enrichment, were non-negotiable and that it would not move to the next phase of the agreement until all key provisions were fully implemented.

Annamalai says he stands by 'DMK Files' allegations after defamation suit withdrawnK Annamalai said DMK MP TR Baalu has withdrawn the Rs 100 crore defamation case linked to his DMK Files allegations. He said he stands by every claim and noted the development comes as his 'We the Leaders' movement moves towards becoming a political party.

Nayara Energy cuts petrol price by Rs 5, diesel by Rs 3 as global oil rates coolNayara Energy has cut petrol prices by Rs 5 per litre and diesel by Rs 3 per litre across its nationwide retail network, becoming the first fuel retailer to reduce prices in over two years. The move follows a decline in global crude oil prices after easing tensions in West Asia.

Another T20I. Same question. Will India hand Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut?England vs India, 1st T20I preview: As Shreyas Iyer's wounded India look to bounce back from a shock series defeat in Ireland, a powerhouse England side awaits in Durham with the clamour growing to hand 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut.

Hindu priest, law student kidnapped, tortured for ransom in DhakaThe man, identified as Subhash Deuri, was admitted to the emergency department of Dhaka Medical College Hospital on Tuesday with serious injuries after friends found him lying on a road in the city's Narinda area.

Jai Hind from an ethanol guinea pigIndia's biggest experiment isn't happening in a laboratory. It's happening at fuel pumps, with 1.4 billion people as the sample. Welcome to the world's biggest fuel trial, where every Indian motorist is unwillingly, a participant.

Long-term live-in implies consent, refusal to marry not rape: Chhattisgarh HCThe Chhattisgarh High Court upheld a trial court's acquittal of a man in a rape and unnatural sex case arising from a live-in relationship, holding that a long-term live-in relationship gives rise to a presumption of consent and that a subsequent refusal to marry, by itself, does not amount to rape.

Samantha's new baby bump pic comes with cheeky note: My six pack, I'll see youSamantha Ruth Prabhu shared a baby bump photo along with a note for her six-pack on Instagram. She is due for delivery later this year.

Tamil Nadu approaches Supreme Court against complete cow slaughter banThe Tamil Nadu government has approached the Supreme Court challenging a Madras High Court order imposing a blanket ban on cow slaughter, arguing that the High Court's directive goes beyond the legal framework and seeks relief.

Trump earned over $1.4 billion from crypto ventures in 2025, filings showA law from 1978 mandates the President and the Vice President of the US to declare their income as well as their assets. Trump's latest filings detailed the fact that his companies received almost USD 800 million from World Liberty Financial, a crypto venture he and his sons co-founded.

Seizures, then zero brain activity: US teen dies after viral Benadryl challengeThe Benadryl challenge first gained traction in 2020 and has resurfaced online in recent weeks. The dangerous trend encourages teenagers to record and upload their attempts on social media platforms after consuming excessive amounts of the antihistamine.

Does choosing family over work still come with an invisible career penalty?Professionals are increasingly questioning whether choosing family over constant workplace visibility hurts their careers. The debate is focusing attention on gaps between flexible work policies and office cultures that still reward presence.

Did you check Siya's background, Ketan Agarwal had asked family: PoliceKetan also complained that Siya's phone was often engaged whenever he called her and that she frequently mentioned Chetan during their conversations.

Brace for heavy rains: Monsoon to drench North India, next 15 days crucialMeteorologists expect a monsoon revival to bring widespread rain across India, including northwestern regions. The shift could aid kharif sowing, replenish reservoirs and ease intense heat, even as flood risks remain.

Satellite captures 1,500-km-long monsoon rain band from Bengal to KashmirSatellite imagery has captured a monsoon trough stretching from the northern Bay of Bengal to Jammu and Kashmir. Its expected southward shift could boost thunderstorms and help the monsoon advance across north India.

sports.headline: Mexico secure first knockout win in 40 years, oust Ecuador to reach last 16 Azteca was rocking long before kick-off and the celebrations continued well after the final whistle as Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 to register their first FIFA World Cup knockout victory in 40 years and book a place in the last 16.

Another T20I. Same question. Will India hand Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut? England vs India, 1st T20I preview: As Shreyas Iyer's wounded India look to bounce back from a shock series defeat in Ireland, a powerhouse England side awaits in Durham with the clamour growing to hand 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his debut.

Serena Williams faces backlash after cancelling press conference at Wimbledon Serena Williams came under fire on social media after cancelling her post-match press conference following a heartbreaking first-round exit at The Championships, Wimbledon on Tuesday.

Mbappe prioritises titles over Lionel Messi's all-time scoring record Kylian Mbappe brushed aside talk of Lionel Messi's World Cup scoring record after France's win over Sweden, insisting his only focus is leading Les Bleus to the FIFA World Cup final on July 19.

Henry explains why Olise, not Mbappe, is France's most important player France great Thierry Henry believes Michael Olise has become Les Bleus' most important player after the Bayern Munich star starred in their 3-0 win over Sweden in the FIFA World Cup Round of 32. Kylian Mbappe scored twice to send France into the last 16, but Olise's two assists and all-round display stole the show.

Emotional Wawrinka fights back tears after final Wimbledon farewell Wimbledon: Stan Wawrinka bid an emotional farewell after a battling four-set loss to Matteo Berrettini, fighting back tears as the crowd rose to applaud the three-time Grand Slam champion in his final appearance at the All England Club.

MEX 2-0 ECU Live: Clinical Mexico reach R16, Ecuador knocked out Mexico (MEX) vs Ecuador (ECU) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico have won their first knockout game in 40 years. They beat Ecuador 2-0 in the R32 match and qualify for the next round without conceding a single goal. Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez's goals are enough for the co-hosts.

Koeman calls time on Netherlands managerial tenure after R32 World Cup exit Ronald Koeman has stepped down as the Netherlands head coach after the Dutch were knocked out by Morocco in the FIFA World Cup Round of 32, ending his second spell in charge after a disappointing campaign.

Mbappe makes World Cup knockouts his own as France crush Sweden in Round of 32 FIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe scored twice to extend his World Cup knockout scoring record as France beat Sweden 3-0 to reach the Round of 16, setting up a clash with Paraguay while moving within one goal of Lionel Messi's all-time tally.

Serena Williams loses on Wimbledon return, but daughter sees a champion's grit Serena Williams' long-awaited Grand Slam return ended in a three-set loss to Maya Joint, but the 23-time major champion reminded Centre Court of her trademark resilience before turning her focus to doubles.

'What's the point of VAR?' Drogba fumes after Ivory Coast's World Cup exit FIFA World Cup 2026: Didier Drogba blasted the officials after Ivory Coast were denied a penalty in their 2-1 World Cup defeat to Norway. The VAR call overshadowed Erling Haaland's late winner and Ivory Coast's exit.

FRA 3-0 SWE Highlights: Mbappe brace powers France through to Round of 16France (FRA) vs Sweden (SWE) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (FRA 3-0 SWE) After a barrage of chances by France to open the scoring, Kylian Mbappe finally opens the scoring at the stroke of half-time. Barcola doubled the lead in the second half. Mbappe makes it a brace as France completes a rout of Sweden in New York.

Haaland has last laugh as Norway spoil Diallo's dazzling display for Ivory CoastFIFA World Cup 2026: Amad Diallo's sensational cameo looked set to inspire Ivory Coast to a famous comeback before Erling Haaland struck late to send Norway into the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16. Norway will now face five-time champions Brazil in a blockbuster knockout clash.

Ashleigh Gardner stars as Australia reaches yet another World Cup finalWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Australia overwhelmed West Indies at The Oval to reach another Women's T20 World Cup final. Beth Mooney and Ashleigh Gardner turned a semi-final into routine, reinforcing Australia's knockout dominance.

LeBron James leaves Lakers after eight seasons, set to test NBA free agencyLeBron James has informed the Lakers that he will continue his NBA career elsewhere, ending an eight-year spell in Los Angeles as the Warriors, Heat and Cavaliers emerge as potential destinations.

Shelton crashes out after five-set thriller as Swiatek scrapes into next roundWimbledon 2026: Otto Virtanen stunned Ben Shelton in a five-set Wimbledon first-round epic, while Iga Swiatek survived Taylor Townsend. The Finn claimed a career-best win as the champion exposed nerves but advanced.

CIV 1-2 NOR Highlights: Erling Haaland sends Norway throughCote d'Ivoire (CIV) vs Norway (NOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (CIV 1-2 NOR) Norway survived a fierce Ivory Coast fightback to punch their ticket to the Round of 16. Now comes the ultimate test as Antonio Nusa, Erling Haaland and company prepare to take on five-time champions Brazil.

Not just Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Shreyas Iyer says India seniors deserved backingENG vs IND: Shreyas Iyer said India's World Cup-winning seniors deserve continued backing ahead of the England T20I series. His remarks came as calls grow for teenager Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to make his long-awaited India debut.

Day 2 Highlights: Serena Williams goes down fighting in comeback gameWimbledon 2026 Day 2 Highlights: Maya Joint spoilt Serena Williams' long-awaited return to professional tennis at Wimbledon, defeating the 23-time Grand Slam champion 6-3, 6-7(6-8), 6-3 in a gripping three-set contest. After Williams battled back to force a decider, the 20-year-old Australian kept her composure in the final set to complete one of the biggest wins of her career.

Hardik Pandya shifts to Bengaluru, makes BCCI facility permanent training baseHardik Pandya has relocated to Bengaluru to train regularly at the BCCI's Centre of Excellence. The move marks an unusual long-term shift as he continues rehabilitation from injury and plans his white-ball future.

Ben Stokes didn't inform Brendon McCullum of retirement until last minuteBen Stokes reportedly told Brendon McCullum about his retirement only on Sunday morning, moments before the England squad was informed. The all-rounder announced his retirement midway through a Test match against England, which ended on Monday. India T

What's wrong with Sooryavanshi carrying drinks? R Ashwin hits back at criticsThe Indian team was criticised for not handing 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi his T20I debut against Ireland. Ahead of the 5-match England series, Ashwin asks, why is it so bad that the youngster was made to carry drinks during the tour.

Shashank Singh denies assault allegations: Cook was taking private videosPunjab Kings cricketer Shashank Singh has denied allegations of assault and wrongful confinement made by his former cook. A day after an FIR was registered against him and his father in Bhopal, Shashank responded to the allegations publicly.

Day 2 schedule: Serena Williams returns to Centre Court, centre stageSerena Williams returns to Centre Court against Maya Joint as Wimbledon's Day 2 schedule takes shape. Her comeback headlines a packed slate featuring Iga Swiatek, Alexander Zverev and Elena Rybakina.

Noisy Mexico fans ruin Ecuador's sleep ahead of knockout clashHundreds of Mexico supporters gathered outside Ecuador's team hotel in Mexico City ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 32 clash against the co-hosts. The rowdy fans created noise with chants, horns and speakers in an apparent attempt to disrupt the visitors' sleep.

Defiant Koeman blasts reporters after Netherlands crash out in Round of 32Netherlands coach Ronald Koeman launched a strong defence of his tactical approach despite his side's FIFA World Cup Round of 32 exit against Morocco. Netherlands crashed out of the tournament after failing to convert in the penalty shootouts.

Watch: Djokovic's violin joke goes viral as daughter Tara can't stop smilingWimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic's violin celebration returned after his Wimbledon win, prompting a shy reaction from daughter Tara. The light-hearted moment followed a testing four-set victory over Wu Yibing in the opening round.

Penalties haunt Netherlands once again as Dutch crash out in R32Penalty shootouts haunted Netherlands once again as Ronald Koeman's team were knocked out of the FIFA World Cup 2026. A disastrous spell of spot kicks saw them miss 3 chances, sealing their fate in the tournament.

Explained: Did VAR call send Germany crashing out of FIFA World Cup?FIFA World Cup 2026: Germany believed Jonathan Tah had sent them through to the Round of 16 before VAR intervened dramatically in extra time. The overturned goal sparked fury, scrutiny of goalkeeper protection, and another painful early tournament exit for the four-time champions.

Harmanpreet Kaur to captain India as BCCI announces Asian Games squadHarmanpreet Kaur will continue to lead India in T20Is. The BCCI on Tuesday announced a 15-member squad for women's cricket at Asian Games 2026, scheduled to be held in Aichi-Nagoya, Japan, in September 2026.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
